title: PPM1H Purification from E. coli
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"79t49","text":"This protocol is used to purify full length PPM1H enzyme in good yield and purity after expression in bacteria.  We generally obtain 2mg from 4L bacterial culture and express the protein at 18°C to avoid aggregation due to overexpression.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Pick a fresh colony from an LB agar plate to inoculate 50 mL LB broth. Grow Overnight with rotation at 37 °C
Autoclave 2 x 6L flasks, each containing 2 L LB broth. Store at 37 °C for inoculation the following day
Inoculate each 6L flask with 20 mL of starter culture
Add 2 mL , 1000X Carbenicillin and 2 mL , 1000X chloramphenicol to flask and rotate at 37 °C until OD 600 0.5-0.6 is reached
Take an initial OD measurement 1hr post inoculation
Doubling time is 20-30min. Calculate how long to reach 0.6 (usually 2.5 hr)
Optional: use remaining O/N culture to make additional bacterial glycerol stocks
Glycerol stocks are made using 500 µL of bacterial culture and 500 µL of 50% Glycerol
Transfer to -80 °C immediately
Once OD is reached, transfer flasks to 18 °C controlled temperature shaker and equilibrate temperature for 10-20min
Add IPTG to 0.3 millimolar (mM)
Incubate with rotation overnight (180 RPM) at 18 °C
Resuspension and Lysis
Transfer cultures to 1L centrifuge bottles
Pellet bacteria by spinning at 4000 RPM in a Sorvall centrifuge for 00:20:00 at 4 °C ; discard supernatant
Add 1 Roche protease inhibitor tablet to 50mL of lysis buffer, rotate ~10 min at 4 °C to dissolve
Set up a ~200mL beaker with a magnetic stir bar at 4 °C
Resuspend pelleted bacteria on ice or in cold room using ~ 10 mL lysis buffer per bottle
Vortex the bottles to resuspend the pellet
Transfer slurry to 200ml beaker with stir bar
Pass 20 mL lysis buffer through the Emulsiflex at 0-30K PSI to equilibrate machine
Pass bacterial slurry through Emulsiflex once at 40-60K PSI to lyse cells. Collect into Oakridge tube(s)
If the slurry resists pipetting up and down, add additional lysis buffer (no more than 20 mL more)
Balance tubes
Centrifuge at 40,000 RPM for 45min in a 45Ti rotor at 4 °C to pellet cell debris
Collect supernatant and pass through a 500ml, 0.45µm Millipore filter under vacuum. Transfer the clarified supernatant into a 50ml glass beaker
HiTrap Talon (Cobalt) Affinity Purification using FPLC
Wash a 1mL HiTrap Talon column with 10 column volumes (CV) of distilled degassed H 2 O
Equilibrate column with 10CV of lysis buffer
Pass the lysate through the column. Collect the flow through
Wash the column with 30CV of wash buffer. Collect the wash fractions
Elute using an imidazole gradient of 100mM-500mM. Collect the elution fractions and analyze by SDS PAGE
Pool the eluted fractions containing the desired protein and dialyze overnight into dialysis buffer in the presence of SUMO protease (Ulp1) (100ng/1mg of protein). We usually dialyze ~6mL

Collect the dialyzed protein the next day and concentrate it by microfiltration on an Centricon 10K cutoff filter before resolving the protein on a S200 24 ml column fitted in series with a 1ml HiTrap Talon column to remove uncleaved His SUMO protein and the His-tagged SUMO protease
Collect 4-5, 4ml fractions. Analyze by SDS-PAGE and pool the fractions containing the desired protein
Concentrate the protein by Centricon filtration if needed
Snap freeze in liquid nitrogen and store at -80 °C
Shown below are SDS-PAGE gels of the initial Cobalt column and gel filtration fractions after His-SUMO tag removal SDS-PAGE of column fractions from this protocol; yield is ~2mg/4L culture
creator: Suzanne R Pfeffer
